svg.text: ESP32 LED Controller v6 — Proper Schematic / Pin-Explicit Bench Reference
Key correction: SN74AHCT125 /OE pins are active-low. Pins 10 and 13 go to +5 V to DISABLE unused channels 3 and 4.

text.tspan: RP1 = MOSFET ideal-diode or other part rated above fuse.
1N5822 is OK only for tiny/current-limited prototype, not final 7.5 A path.

svg.text: P5_LOGIC_USB (+5 V from ESP32 USB/VIN only)
SN74AHCT125 OE truth: /OE = LOW enables A→Y; /OE = HIGH disables output to high-Z. Therefore used pins 1 & 4 go to GND; unused pins 10 & 13 go to +5 V.
If pins 10/13 were tied to GND, channels 3/4 would be enabled; with inputs tied GND and outputs NC it usually would not damage anything, but it is not the clean disabled-state schematic.
